title: The Only Way to Save Europe
gist: Molinari argues, per the article's own subtitle, that Europe's only way to survive mounting external pressure is to 'act like a country' — i.e., unify politically — citing Pentagon pressure for Europe to take over NATO leadership by 2027, fallout from the U.S.-Iran war, and rising Russian and Chinese pressure; the accessible pre-paywall text ends mid-sentence before detailing his prescription.

insights.claim: In December 2025, Pentagon officials told European diplomats that Europe must assume leadership of NATO by 2027, a signal that the transatlantic alliance's traditional U.S.-led structure may be coming to an end.
insights.speaker: Maurizio Molinari
insights.so_what: A concrete, dated signal (2027 deadline) that Europe needs to rapidly build independent defense capacity — relevant for European defense-sector positioning.
insights.quote: the continent must assume leadership of NATO by 2027
insights.locator: In December 2025, Pentagon officials told European diplomats

insights.claim: The Trump administration's decision to go to war with Iran without serious consultation with European allies produced a global energy crisis and deepened European doubts about U.S. reliability as a security partner.
insights.speaker: Maurizio Molinari
insights.so_what: Ties a specific U.S. military decision directly to a global energy shock and evidence of eroding U.S.-Europe trust that could accelerate European strategic-autonomy moves.
insights.quote: Washington’s decision to go to war with Iran, made without serious consultation with its European allies, has produced a global energy crisis
insights.locator: Meanwhile, Washington’s decision to go to war with Iran

insights.claim: Europe simultaneously faces growing Russian military aggression and Chinese commercial and technological pressure, compounding its economic and security challenges alongside the NATO and U.S.-reliability issues.
insights.speaker: Maurizio Molinari
insights.so_what: Frames Europe as facing a three-front squeeze (U.S. retrenchment, Russian military threat, Chinese economic/tech competition) simultaneously — relevant for European risk premium and defense/industrial-policy bets.
insights.quote: growing Russian military aggression and Chinese commercial and technological pressure pose serious economic and security problems for Europe

insights.claim: Molinari's thesis, stated in the article's own subtitle, is that the only way for Europe to survive this convergence of pressures is to 'act like a country,' implying a call for greater EU political and institutional unification, though the specific mechanism is not covered in the paywall-limited text.
insights.speaker: Maurizio Molinari
insights.so_what: Flags the article's core policy recommendation (EU federalization/unification) for follow-up once the full text is available — relevant framing for EU-integration-linked trades.
insights.quote: The Continent Must Act Like a Country
insights.locator: The Only Way to Save Europe / The Continent Must Act Like a Country
